name: Mega Sol
effect: Even when the sunlight has not turned harsh, the Pokémon can use its moves as if the weather were harsh sunlight.

name: Dragonize
effect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.

name: Piercing Drill
effect: When the Pokémon uses contact moves, it can hit even targets that are protecting themselves, dealing 1/4 of the damage that the move would otherwise deal. Everything aside from the target's protective effects is still triggered.

name: Spicy Spray
effect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move, it burns the attacker.

name: Eelevate
effect: The Pokémon floats off the ground, making it immune to Ground-type moves, as well as the Spikes, Toxic Spikes, and Sticky Web statuses. When the Pokémon knocks out a target with an attack, its highest stat is boosted by 1 stage.

name: Fire Mane
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon’s Fire-type moves by 50%.

name: Adaptability
effect: Boosts the power of moves of the same type as the Pokémon by 100% instead of 50%.

name: Aerilate
effect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Flying-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.

name: Aftermath
effect: Attackers that knock out the Pokémon with a contact move take damage equal to 1/4 of their max HP.

name: Analytic
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's moves by 30% when the Pokémon is the last to move that turn.

name: Anger Point
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat to its sixth stage when the Pokémon takes a critical hit.

name: Anticipation
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it senses whether the opponents know any one-hit KO moves or moves that are super effective against it.

name: Armor Tail
effect: Opponents are unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.

name: Aroma Veil
effect: The Pokémon and its allies cannot gain the Infatuated, Taunted, Unable to Repeat, Move Disabled, Healing Prevented, or Encore statuses.

name: Battle Armor
effect: Attacks landed on the Pokémon will never be critical hits.

name: Berserk
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat by 1 stage when an attack causes its HP to drop to 1/2 or less of its max.

name: Big Pecks
effect: The Pokémon's Defense stat cannot be lowered by other Pokémon's moves or Abilities.

name: Blaze
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's Fire-type moves by 50% when its HP drops to 1/3 or less of its max.

name: Bulletproof
effect: The Pokémon is immune to ball and bomb moves.

name: Cheek Pouch
effect: The Pokémon has 1/3 of its max HP restored when it eats a Berry, in addition to the Berry's usual effect.

name: Chlorophyll
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed stat in harsh sunlight.

name: Clear Body
effect: The Pokémon's stats cannot be lowered by other Pokémon's moves or Abilities.

name: Cloud Nine
effect: Eliminates the effects of weather.

name: Competitive
effect: When the Pokémon has any of its stats lowered by an opponent, its Sp. Atk stat is boosted by 2 stages.

name: Compound Eyes
effect: Boosts the accuracy of the Pokémon's moves by 30%.

name: Contrary
effect: Reverses any stat changes affecting the Pokémon so that attempts to boost its stats instead lower them—and attempts to lower its stats will boost them.

name: Corrosion
effect: The Pokémon can poison or badly poison targets even if they're Steel or Poison types.

name: Cud Chew
effect: If the Pokémon eats a Berry, it will eat that same Berry once more at the end of the next turn.

name: Curious Medicine
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it removes all stat changes from its allies.

name: Cursed Body
effect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move, the attacker has a 30% chance of gaining the Move Disabled status for 4 turns.

name: Cute Charm
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has a 30% chance of gaining the Infatuated status if the attacker and the Pokémon are of opposite genders.

name: Damp
effect: All Pokémon become unable to use explosive moves. Explosive Abilities also fail to trigger.

name: Defiant
effect: When the Pokémon has any of its stats lowered by an opponent, its Attack stat is boosted by 2 stages.

name: Disguise
effect: When the Pokémon is in its Disguised Form and would take damage from a move, it loses 1/8 of its max HP instead of taking the damage, then changes into its Busted Form.

name: Drizzle
effect: Summons rain for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Drought
effect: Summons harsh sunlight for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Dry Skin
effect: Water-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they restore 1/4 of its max HP. However, the Pokémon takes 25% more damage from Fire-type moves. The Pokémon has 1 /8 of its max HP restored at the end of every turn in rain, but it loses 1/8 of its max HP at the end of every turn in harsh sunlight.

name: Early Bird
effect: The Pokémon awakens from sleep twice as fast as other Pokémon.

name: Earth Eater
effect: Ground-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they restore 1/4 of its max HP.

name: Effect Spore
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has a 30% chance of being poisoned, paralyzed, or put to sleep.

name: Electric Surge
effect: Turns the entire field into Electric Terrain for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Electromorphosis
effect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move, it gains the Electric Boost status, which doubles the power of the next Electric-type move it uses.

name: Fairy Aura
effect: Boosts the power of the Fairy-type moves of all Pokémon on the field by 33%.

name: Filter
effect: Reduces the damage the Pokémon takes from super-effective moves by 25%.

name: Flame Body
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has a 30% chance of being burned.

name: Flash Fire
effect: Fire-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they give the Pokémon the Flash Fire status, which boosts the power of Fire-type moves by 50%.

name: Flower Veil
effect: Grass-type allies are immune to status conditions and cannot have their stats lowered.

name: Fluffy
effect: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes from contact moves, but doubles the damage taken from Fire-type moves.

name: Forecast
effect: The Pokémon transforms with the weather to change its type to Water, Fire, or Ice.

name: Forewarn
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it identifies the move with the highest power among all the moves the opponents know.

name: Friend Guard
effect: Reduces the damage allies take by 25%.

name: Frisk
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it identifies opponents' held items.

name: Fur Coat
effect: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes from physical moves.

name: Gale Wings
effect: Increases the priority of the Pokémon's Flying-type moves by 1 stage while its HP is full.

name: Gluttony
effect: If the Pokémon is holding a Berry to be eaten when its HP drops to 1/4 or less of its max, it will instead eat the Berry when its HP drops to 1/2 or less of its max.

name: Good As Gold
effect: The Pokémon is immune to status moves used by other Pokémon.

name: Gooey
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker's Speed stat is lowered by 1 stage.

name: Guts
effect: When the Pokémon has a status condition, its Attack stat is boosted by 50%. Being burned does not halve the damage dealt by the Pokémon's physical moves.

name: Harvest
effect: If the Pokémon has used a Berry, it has a 50% chance of creating another one at the end of every turn. In harsh sunlight, the Pokémon will definitely create a Berry.

name: Healer
effect: The Pokémon has a 50% chance of curing the status conditions of its allies at the end of every turn.

name: Heatproof
effect: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes from Fire-type moves and from being burned.

name: Heavy Metal
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's weight.

name: Hospitality
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it restores 1/4 of its ally's max HP.

name: Huge Power
effect: Doubles the power of the Pokémon's physical moves.

name: Hunger Switch
effect: The Pokémon changes its form, alternating between its Full Belly Mode and Hangry Mode at the end of every turn.

name: Hustle
effect: When the Pokémon uses physical moves, its Attack stat is boosted by 50%, but its accuracy is lowered by 20%.

name: Hydration
effect: Cures the Pokémon's status conditions at the end of every turn in rain.

name: Hyper Cutter
effect: The Pokémon's Attack stat cannot be lowered by other Pokémon's moves or Abilities.

name: Ice Body
effect: The Pokémon has 1/16 of its max HP restored at the end of every turn in snow.

name: Illuminate
effect: The Pokémon ignores changes to targets' evasiveness and its accuracy cannot be lowered.

name: Illusion
effect: The Pokémon enters battle disguised as the last Pokémon in its party. It reverts to its usual appearance when it takes damage from a move.

name: Immunity
effect: The Pokémon cannot be poisoned or badly poisoned.

name: Imposter
effect: The Pokémon transforms into the Pokémon in front of it. It also copies all of that Pokémon's stats apart from its HP.

name: Infiltrator
effect: When using its moves, the Pokémon ignores the effects of targets' Light Screen, Reflect, Aurora Veil, Safeguard, and substitutes.

name: Innards Out
effect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move that knocks it out, it deals the same amount of damage to the attacker.

name: Inner Focus
effect: The Pokémon never flinches when attacked and is unaffected by Intimidate.

name: Insomnia
effect: The Pokémon cannot become drowsy or be put to sleep.

name: Intimidate
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it lowers the Attack stats of opponents by 1 stage.

name: Iron Fist
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's punching moves by 20%.

name: Justified
effect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a Dark-type move, its Attack stat is boosted by 1 stage.

name: Keen Eye
effect: The Pokémon ignores changes to targets' evasiveness and its accuracy cannot be lowered.

name: Klutz
effect: Items do not work when held by the Pokémon.

name: Leaf Guard
effect: The Pokémon is immune to status conditions in harsh sunlight.

name: Levitate
effect: The Pokémon floats off the ground, making it immune to Ground-type moves, as well as the Spikes, Toxic Spikes, and Sticky Web statuses.

name: Light Metal
effect: Halves the Pokémon's weight.

name: Lightning Rod
effect: The Pokémon draws in all Electric-type moves. These moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they boost its Sp. Atk stat by 1 stage.

name: Limber
effect: The Pokémon cannot be paralyzed.

name: Liquid Voice
effect: The Pokémon's sound-based moves become Water-type moves.

name: Long Reach
effect: None of the moves used by the Pokémon are considered contact moves.

name: Magic Bounce
effect: Instead of being affected by other Pokémon's status moves, the Pokémon bounces them back at the user.

name: Magic Guard
effect: The Pokémon takes damage only from attacks.

name: Magician
effect: If the Pokémon is not already holding an item, it will steal the held item from targets it deals damage to with its moves.

name: Magma Armor
effect: The Pokémon cannot be frozen.

name: Marvel Scale
effect: When the Pokémon has a status condition, its Defense stat is boosted by 50%.

name: Mega Launcher
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's pulse moves by 50%.

name: Merciless
effect: The Pokémon's attacks become critical hits if the target is poisoned or badly poisoned.

name: Mimicry
effect: The Pokémon's type changes depending on the terrain.

name: Minus
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat by 50% if an ally with the Plus or Minus Ability is also in battle.

name: Mirror Armor
effect: Instead of being affected by stat-lowering effects, the Pokémon bounces them back at whichever Pokémon caused them.

name: Mold Breaker
effect: The Pokémon's moves are unaffected by the Ability of the target (with certain exceptions).

name: Moody
effect: At the end of every turn, one of the Pokémon's stats will be boosted by 2 stages, but another will be lowered by 1 stage.

name: Motor Drive
effect: Electric-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they boost its Speed stat by 1 stage.

name: Moxie
effect: When the Pokémon knocks out a target with an attack, its Attack stat is boosted by 1 stage.

name: Multiscale
effect: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes while its HP is full.

name: Mummy
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has its Ability changed to Mummy.

name: Natural Cure
effect: The Pokémon's status conditions are cured when it switches out of battle.

name: No Guard
effect: The accuracy of moves used both by and against the Pokémon becomes 100%.

name: Oblivious
effect: The Pokémon cannot gain the Infatuated or Taunted statuses and is unaffected by Intimidate.

name: Opportunist
effect: When an opponent's stats are boosted, the Pokémon boosts its own stats in the exact same way.

name: Overcoat
effect: The Pokémon takes no damage from sandstorms and is immune to moves and Abilities involving powder.

name: Overgrow
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's Grass-type moves by 50% when its HP drops to 1/3 or less of its max.

name: Own Tempo
effect: The Pokémon cannot become confused and is unaffected by Intimidate.

name: Parental Bond
effect: The parent and child attack one after the other. The power of the child's attacks is 1/4 of those of the parent.

name: Pickpocket
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, it will steal the held item of the attacker if it is not already holding an item.

name: Pickup
effect: If the Pokémon is not already holding an item, at the end of the turn it will pick up an item that was consumed by another Pokémon.

name: Pixilate
effect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Fairy-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.

name: Plus
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat by 50% if an ally with the Plus or Minus Ability is also in battle.

name: Poison Heal
effect: If poisoned or badly poisoned, the Pokémon has 1/8 of its max HP restored at the end of every turn instead of losing HP.

name: Poison Point
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has a 30% chance of being poisoned.

name: Poison Touch
effect: When the Pokémon hits a target with a contact move, the target has a 30% chance of being poisoned.

name: Prankster
effect: Increases the priority of the Pokémon's status moves by 1 stage.

name: Pressure
effect: Causes opponents to expend 1 more PP when using moves against the Pokémon.

name: Protean
effect: Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use. This works only once per time the Pokémon enters battle.

name: Pure Power
effect: Doubles the power of the Pokémon's physical moves.

name: Purifying Salt
effect: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes from Ghost-type moves. The Pokémon is immune to status conditions.

name: Queenly Majesty
effect: Opponents are unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.

name: Quick Draw
effect: The Pokémon's moves have a 30% chance of going first among moves of the same priority.

name: Quick Feet
effect: When the Pokémon has a status condition, its Speed stat is boosted by 50%. Being paralyzed does not lower the Pokémon's Speed stat.

name: Rain Dish
effect: The Pokémon has 1/16 of its max HP restored at the end of every turn in rain.

name: Receiver
effect: The Pokémon changes its Ability to match that of a defeated ally.

name: Reckless
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's moves by 20% if they have recoil or crash damage.

name: Refrigerate
effect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Ice-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.

name: Regenerator
effect: The Pokémon has 1/3 of its max HP restored when it switches out of battle.

name: Ripen
effect: Doubles the effects of Berries eaten by the Pokémon.

name: Rivalry
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's moves by 25% against targets of the same gender, and lowers it by 25% against targets of the opposite gender. The power remains the same as usual if either Pokémon's gender is unknown.

name: Rock Head
effect: The Pokémon will not lose HP due to recoil damage from its moves.

name: Rough Skin
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker takes damage equal to 1/8 of its max HP.

name: Sand Force
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's Rock-, Ground-, and Steel-type moves by 30% in a sandstorm.

name: Sand Rush
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed stat in a sandstorm.

name: Sand Spit
effect: Summons a sandstorm for 5 turns when the Pokémon takes damage from moves.

name: Sand Stream
effect: Summons a sandstorm for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Sand Veil
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness by 25% in a sandstorm.

name: Sap Sipper
effect: Grass-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they boost its Attack stat by 1 stage.

name: Scrappy
effect: The Pokémon can hit Ghost types with Normal- and Fighting-type moves. It is also unaffected by Intimidate.

name: Screen Cleaner
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it removes the Light Screen, Reflect, and Aurora Veil statuses.

name: Shadow Tag
effect: Opponents cannot be switched out of battle.

name: Sharpness
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's slicing moves by 50%.

name: Shed Skin
effect: The Pokémon has a 30% chance of curing its own status conditions at the end of every turn.

name: Sheer Force
effect: The Pokémon's moves lose their additional effects, but the power of those moves will be boosted by 30%.

name: Shell Armor
effect: Attacks landed on the Pokémon will never be critical hits.

name: Shield Dust
effect: The Pokémon is immune to additional effects from attacks.

name: Skill Link
effect: The Pokémon's multistrike moves always hit the maximum number of times.

name: Slush Rush
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed stat in snow.

name: Sniper
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's critical hits by 125% instead of 50%.

name: Snow Cloak
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness by 25% in snow.

name: Snow Warning
effect: Summons snow for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Solar Power
effect: In harsh sunlight, the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat is boosted by 50%, but it loses 1/8 of its max HP at the end of every turn.

name: Solid Rock
effect: Reduces the damage the Pokémon takes from super-effective moves by 25%.

name: Soundproof
effect: The Pokémon is immune to sound-based moves.

name: Speed Boost
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat by 1 stage at the end of every turn.

name: Stall
effect: The Pokémon's moves go last among moves of the same priority.

name: Stalwart
effect: The Pokémon ignores the effects of Abilities and moves that draw in moves.

name: Stamina
effect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move, its Defense stat is boosted by 1 stage.

name: Stance Change
effect: The Pokémon changes into its Blade Forme when it attacks and changes into its Shield Forme when it uses the move King's Shield.

name: Static
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has a 30% chance of being paralyzed.

name: Steadfast
effect: When the Pokémon flinches, its Speed stat is boosted by 1 stage.

name: Stench
effect: When the Pokémon deals damage with its moves, there is a 10% chance that targets will flinch.

name: Sticky Hold
effect: The Pokémon's held item cannot be stolen or removed by other Pokémon.

name: Strong Jaw
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's biting moves by 50%.

name: Sturdy
effect: If the Pokémon has full HP and takes damage from a move that would knock it out in one hit, it will endure the hit with 1 HP. The Pokémon is also immune to one-hit KO moves.

name: Suction Cups
effect: The Pokémon is unaffected by the moves and held items of other Pokémon that would force it to switch out of battle.

name: Super Luck
effect: The Pokémon has a 1-stage Critical-Hit Ratio Boost.

name: Supersweet Syrup
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, opponents' evasiveness is lowered by 1 stage. This Ability is triggered only once per battle.

name: Supreme Overlord
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, the power of its moves is boosted by 10% for each Pokémon in its party that has been defeated in the battle already. The maximum boost is 50%.

name: Surge Surfer
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed stat on Electric Terrain.

name: Swarm
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's Bug-type moves by 50% when its HP drops to 1/3 or less of its max.

name: Sweet Veil
effect: The Pokémon and its allies cannot become drowsy or be put to sleep.

name: Swift Swim
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed stat in rain.

name: Symbiosis
effect: When an ally consumes an item, the Pokémon gives its own held item to that ally.

name: Synchronize
effect: If the Pokémon is burned, paralyzed, poisoned, or badly poisoned by another Pokémon's move or Ability, that Pokémon will also be inflicted with the same status condition.

name: Tangled Feet
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's evasiveness if it is confused.

name: Technician
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's moves by 50% if their power is 60 or less.

name: Telepathy
effect: The Pokémon dodges attacks from its allies.

name: Thick Fat
effect: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes from Fire- and Ice-type moves.

name: Torrent
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's Water-type moves by 50% when its HP drops to 1/3 or less of its max.

name: Tough Claws
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's contact moves by 30%.

name: Toxic Debris
effect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a physical move, it gives the opponent's side the Toxic Spikes status, which sets Toxic Spikes on their field.

name: Trace
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it changes its Ability to match that of an opponent.

name: Unaware
effect: The Pokémon ignores the target's stat changes when attacking, and ignores the attacker's stat changes when being attacked. It cannot ignore changes to the Speed stat, however.

name: Unburden
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed stat when its held item is consumed or lost.

name: Unnerve
effect: Makes opponents unable to eat Berries.

name: Unseen Fist
effect: When the Pokémon uses contact moves, it can hit even targets that are protecting themselves, dealing 1/4 of the damage that the move would otherwise deal. Everything aside from the target's protective effects is still triggered.

name: Vital Spirit
effect: The Pokémon cannot become drowsy or be put to sleep.

name: Volt Absorb
effect: Electric-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they restore 1/4 of its max HP.

name: Wandering Spirit
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, it swaps Abilities with the attacker.

name: Water Absorb
effect: Water-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they restore 1/4 of its max HP.

name: Water Bubble
effect: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes from Fire-type moves and doubles the power of its Water-type moves. The Pokémon cannot be burned.

name: Weak Armor
effect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a physical move, its Defense stat is lowered by 1 stage, but its Speed stat is boosted by 2 stages.

name: White Smoke
effect: The Pokémon's stats cannot be lowered by other Pokémon's moves or Abilities.

name: Zero To Hero
effect: The Pokémon changes into its Hero Form when it switches out of battle.
